executive_summary: Analyzed 641 articles (535 competitor, 106 internal). See differentiators and reader advantages sections for detailed topic analysis.

market_pulse.narrative: A wave of regulatory scrutiny is sweeping the iGaming sector, with new EU tax proposals, intensified Swedish supervisory checks, AGCOM’s crackdown on stealth marketing, and proactive notices from MGA ahead of the World Cup. Operators face tighter compliance demands across multiple jurisdictions.

Prediction markets are emerging as a growth vector for operators like DraftKings, while regulators and courts in the US are testing the legality of such products, exemplified by lawsuits in Rhode Island and statements from Caesars Digital on potential legalization.

Strategic acquisitions and takeover talks dominate headlines, with MGM’s potential deal, Evoke’s bid by Bally’s Intralot, and Barry Diller’s $18 bn proposal for MGM Resorts, signalling a reshaping of the global iGaming landscape.
Operators are confronting the hidden costs of poor affiliate tracking, while also investing in AI‑driven automation and fast‑withdrawal solutions to build trust with affiliates and end‑users.

New partnerships and regional initiatives – from Polymarket’s OneFootball deal to Genlot’s South African lottery launch and the Gaming Tech Summit Africa – illustrate a push into new geographies and product verticals.

strategic_gaps.description: Competitors are publishing extensive analysis of upcoming EU gambling taxes, Swedish regulator checks, AGCOM’s ad ban enforcement, and MGA alerts, while our internal feed contains only generic job postings and no regulatory insight.
strategic_gaps.competitor_coverage: EU tax proposal, Swedish supervisory checks on LeoVegas, bet365 and Unibet, AGCOM crackdown on stealth marketing, MGA notice ahead of World Cup, Sweden’s compliance review of operators.
strategic_gaps.our_coverage: Minimal – internal articles are limited to recruitment notices with no regulatory content.

portfolio_wins.our_narrative: Our portfolio demonstrates a proactive approach to strengthening operational teams, highlighted by recent hires for VIP Account Management and Junior Risk Management, positioning us to better serve high‑value players and manage risk.
Our iGB Affiliate brand is prominently featured in the 2026 Awards shortlist, underscoring our position as a leading affiliate network and thought‑leader in the industry.
Our internal article on an AML & Compliance Officer demonstrates a strong focus on regulatory compliance, aligning with industry‑wide heightened scrutiny.
The piece on opportunities and obstacles for affiliates in South Africa showcases deep regional affiliate knowledge, a niche not covered by competitors in this batch.
Publishing the 2026 shortlist for the iGB Affiliate Awards positions us as a thought leader within the affiliate community.
